title: Dactylorhiza × altobracensis
intro: Dactylorhiza × altobracensis is a hybrid orchid species, resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza sambucina and Dactylorhiza maculata, characterized by its unique flower and growth habits. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in wound healing and soothing skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza × altobracensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Dactylorhiza × altobracensis is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza × altobracensis is a hybrid orchid species. It is classified within the genus Dactylorhiza, which includes terrestrial orchids commonly known as spotted orchids. The species is thought to be a hybrid of Dactylorhiza sambucina and Dactylorhiza fuchsii. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic analysis. The exact classification and taxonomic status of Dactylorhiza × altobracensis may still be subject to ongoing research and review.
uses: Dactylorhiza × altobracensis is a hybrid orchid species, and as such, its medicinal uses are largely unexplored and not well-documented. However, its parent species, Dactylorhiza spp., have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Some species within the Dactylorhiza genus have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
